# Tabucom

Publish HTML, Markdown, or a ZIP containing a prebuilt static site with one HTTP request. {{PUBLISH_AUTH_SUMMARY}} Every deployment is immutable and stops serving at the requested TTL, or after the default 30 days (720 hours) when no TTL is supplied.

## Agent behavior

Publish only the final static artifact that a browser can serve directly. Do not upload project source files.

If the user asks for a specific lifetime, readable URL name, client-side routing support, or visitor protection, apply the matching publish option below. If the user does not ask, publish with the defaults and do not pause for option selection.

## Agent execution rules

1. Use the publish origin shown in this document. Operators should set `PUBLIC_API_URL` so generated instructions match the externally reachable URL.
2. Always publish built static output only. Common build directories: `dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `public/`.
3. If no build output exists, run the project build command only when obvious: `npm run build`, `pnpm build`, `yarn build`.
4. For ZIP publishing:
   - zip the contents of the build directory, not the directory itself.
   - ensure `index.html` exists at ZIP root.
   - use `spa=1` for React, Vue, Vite, SvelteKit static, Angular, or other client-side routers.
5. Never upload: source code directories, `node_modules`, `.git`, secrets, `.env`, or backend/server code.
6. On success, return only: `url`, `expiresAt`, `password` if returned, and `protected`.
7. After success, mention that future publishes can set `ttl`, `prefix`, `spa`, or password protection when useful.
8. On failure: read the HTTP status and response body, explain the cause briefly, and suggest the smallest correction.
{{PUBLISH_TOKEN_INSTRUCTIONS}}

## API

- POST /api/v1/publish
- Optional query `spa=1` enables index.html fallback for client-side routing.
- Optional query `ttl=<duration>` sets deployment lifetime, for example `ttl=72h` or `ttl=90m`.
- Optional query `prefix=<slug>` makes the returned ID start with a readable prefix plus a random 16-hex-character suffix. Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
- Optional header `Tabucom-Password: <password>` sets an 8–128 character printable ASCII visitor password.
- Optional query `generatePassword=1` generates a visitor password. Do not combine it with `Tabucom-Password`.
- Content-Type: `text/html`, `text/markdown`, or `application/zip`.
- GET /healthz checks readiness.
- GET /agents provides the complete human-readable usage guide.
- GET /openapi.json provides the complete machine-readable contract.
- Path-mode content is served at `/p/{id}/`; deployments may instead use `https://{id}.<preview-domain>/` when wildcard hosting is configured. Always use the returned URL.
- Path mode shares the service origin; wildcard preview domains provide stronger browser-origin isolation for untrusted content.
- Add `?raw=1` to a published page URL to retrieve the resolved stored file without Tabucom's browser copy control. For HTML pages, this is the byte-exact source to hand back to an agent.

## Examples

HTML:
`curl -sS -X POST "{{ORIGIN}}/api/v1/publish?ttl=72h&prefix=my-super-website" -H "Content-Type: text/html" --data-binary @index.html`

HTML with a generated password:
`curl -sS -X POST "{{ORIGIN}}/api/v1/publish?generatePassword=1" -H "Content-Type: text/html" --data-binary @index.html`

Markdown:
`curl -sS -X POST "{{ORIGIN}}/api/v1/publish" -H "Content-Type: text/markdown" --data-binary @report.md`

Node static build:
`npm ci && npm run build`
`(cd dist && zip -qr ../site.zip .)`
`curl -sS -X POST "{{ORIGIN}}/api/v1/publish?spa=1&ttl=72h" -H "Content-Type: application/zip" --data-binary @site.zip`

The ZIP must contain a regular `index.html` file at its root. The service never runs Node.js, npm, SSR, server code, APIs, databases, or build commands.

Defaults: 100 MB compressed upload, 500 MB expanded ZIP, 10,000 ZIP entries including directories, 60 publishes/hour/network peer. Dangerous paths, symlinks, conflicting paths, duplicate normalized paths, devices, and archive bombs are rejected.

Every success response includes `protected`; protected responses include `password`. Protected deployments show a single-field form and remember successful access until expiry. Use HTTPS in production. Failed password submissions are limited to 10 per minute per deployment and network peer.
